note: All two-handed melee weapons. Minimum Physiology 20 for all entries. base_dmg_range is [min, max] for Normal Iron quality. Quality and material multipliers applied by engine. base_cost_copper is Normal Iron price.
battle_stamina_note: 2H weapons drain Battle Stamina faster than 1H. At Physiology 20, wielder lasts 5 turns before stamina hits 0. At Physiology 30, 10 turns. Formula: floor((Phys - 10) / 2). At 0 stamina, damage drops 50%.
meteor_hammer_ranged_note: The Meteor Hammer can be thrown to strike a target 2 hexes away (ranged arc throw). Still a one-time use per battle with guaranteed crit when thrown, then retrieved.

weapons.name: Greatsword
weapons.flavor: The standard for professional soldiers who want every strike to count. Heavy, decisive, unforgiving.

weapons.name: Claymore
weapons.flavor: Longer than most, with a crossguard angled forward. Cuts with the full arc of the arm.

weapons.name: Zweihander
weapons.special_rule: On a hit, 15% chance to apply Staggering effect — target pushed back 1 hex and loses initiative this turn.
weapons.flavor: Almost as tall as the person wielding it. Designed to break polearm formations and sweep entire files.

weapons.name: Executioner Sword
weapons.special_rule: On a killing blow against an unarmoured or lightly armoured target, has 25% chance to instantly decapitate — applying immediate death regardless of remaining HP.
weapons.flavor: Not built for battle. Built to end it cleanly, in one stroke. The weight is concentrated at the tip.

weapons.name: Warblade
weapons.flavor: Broad, heavy, and practical. No ceremony — just an instrument of controlled destruction.

weapons.name: Great Axe
weapons.flavor: Enormous single head. Each swing carries enough mass to split plate apart if it finds the right angle.

weapons.name: Double Axe
weapons.special_rule: Twin heads: on a miss, the back-swing counts as a 50% damage secondary attack.
weapons.flavor: Two heads — forward and reverse. One swing has two bites.

weapons.name: Battle Axe
weapons.flavor: Heavy, reliable, and honest about what it does. The standard heavy axe for those who need results.

weapons.name: Greataxe
weapons.special_rule: On a killing blow, deal 50% of excess damage as splash to an adjacent target.
weapons.flavor: Maximum head, maximum shaft, maximum damage. The weapon of chieftains who want the field to notice.

weapons.name: Poleaxe
weapons.special_rule: Three damage modes active on a single weapon. Each hit rolls which face connects first — damage split reflects the result.
weapons.flavor: Axe head, spike, and hammer poll in one. Every angle of a battle has an answer on this shaft.

weapons.name: Halberd
weapons.special_rule: Hook: 20% chance to pull a rider from their mount. Can strike targets 2 hexes away.
weapons.flavor: The answer to cavalry, pike formations, and armoured infantry. A versatile weapon that has no equal on a battlefield line.

weapons.name: Glaive
weapons.special_rule: Can strike all targets in a 180° arc (reach 2) for half damage as a special attack, costing extra Battle Stamina.
weapons.flavor: The long blade at the end of a pole converts momentum into a sweeping cut. Graceful, deadly, and unforgiving of tight spaces.

weapons.name: Long Spear
weapons.special_rule: Brace: if the wielder has not moved this turn, they can ready the spear — any charging enemy takes double damage.
weapons.flavor: Reach is the spear's oldest advantage. Two hexes of safe distance, directed to a point.

weapons.name: Pike
weapons.special_rule: Line weapon: in formation with adjacent ally also using a pike, both gain +15% accuracy.
weapons.flavor: Almost useless alone. In a line of ten, it is one of the most lethal weapons on any battlefield.

weapons.name: Lance
weapons.special_rule: Mounted charge: on horseback, if the wielder moves 2+ hexes in a straight line before hitting, damage is doubled. Without a mount: -30% accuracy.
weapons.flavor: Built for the charge. On horseback it is devastating. On foot it is awkward and heavy.

weapons.name: Great Hammer
weapons.special_rule: Deals 20% bonus damage to Undead. On a crit, applies Concussion.
weapons.flavor: Pure mass applied at the end of a long lever. Nothing about it is subtle.

weapons.name: Maul
weapons.special_rule: Destroys non-magical wooden shields on a critical hit. Deals 20% bonus damage to Undead.
weapons.flavor: A demolition tool. Breaks things apart — doors, shields, ribs. It does not care which.

weapons.name: War Maul
weapons.special_rule: On a hit: 20% chance of Concussion. Destroys non-magical shields on a crit. Deals 20% bonus damage to Undead.
weapons.flavor: When a Maul is not enough, someone builds this. Maximum mass. Minimum mercy.

weapons.name: Spiked Maul
weapons.special_rule: Spikes: 15% chance of Rupturing secondary on a successful hit.
weapons.flavor: The spikes are not decorative. They punch through where the flat face cannot.

weapons.name: Great Mace
weapons.special_rule: On crit: applies Staggering. Deals 15% bonus damage to Undead.
weapons.flavor: Holy orders issue these to the largest and most committed of their warriors. It preaches in blows.

weapons.name: Grand Flail
weapons.special_rule: Bypasses shield AC bonus entirely. On a crit, applies Concussion.
weapons.flavor: The chained head orbits wide and arrives from angles that shields cannot follow.

weapons.name: Chain Flail
weapons.special_rule: Bypass shield AC. 15% chance to entangle target's weapon arm — -20% accuracy next turn.
weapons.flavor: Chain instead of rod — it wraps. What it wraps around rarely comes free cleanly.

weapons.name: Staff
weapons.flavor: The oldest multi-purpose tool. Reach, balance, and enough mass to be genuinely dangerous.

weapons.name: Iron Staff
weapons.flavor: A wooden staff reinforced or fully replaced with iron. Heavier, harder, and very unpleasant to receive.

weapons.name: Weighted Staff
weapons.special_rule: On a hit: 10% chance of Concussion from the concentrated mass at the tip.
weapons.flavor: The weighted end concentrates force. One end for blocking, one end for hitting.

weapons.name: Bladed Staff
weapons.flavor: A blade affixed to a long shaft. Reach of a staff, cutting power of a sword.

weapons.name: Twinblade Staff
weapons.special_rule: Both ends are bladed. On a hit, 20% chance to follow up with a bonus 50% damage backswing from the second blade.
weapons.flavor: Two blades on a single shaft. Each swing ends with another beginning. Hard to defend against and harder to predict.

weapons.name: Scythe
weapons.flavor: Harvesting tool. The curved blade on a long shaft draws back through the target on the return stroke.

weapons.name: War Scythe
weapons.special_rule: Sweep attack: can hit all targets in a 2-hex arc for 60% damage (costs Battle Stamina).
weapons.flavor: The farming scythe reoriented to kill efficiently rather than efficiently harvest. The difference is surprisingly minor.

weapons.name: Reaper Blade
weapons.special_rule: On kill: regain 10% Battle Stamina.
weapons.flavor: Named what it does. The oversized blade on the extended haft reaps through targets without slowing.

weapons.name: Heavy Trident
weapons.special_rule: Three prongs: 25% chance to pin a Large or smaller target on a critical hit (cannot move that turn).
weapons.flavor: The three prongs spread wide enough to catch, trap, and hold. Used where control matters as much as damage.

weapons.name: Pole Pick
weapons.special_rule: Treats target armour class as 3 lower when target wears plate.
weapons.flavor: The extended pick leverages body weight. Even plate armour bends under a properly driven strike.

weapons.name: Heavy Cleaver
weapons.special_rule: On a killing blow, 60% splash damage to one adjacent enemy.
weapons.flavor: The width of the blade is the point. It does not penetrate — it divides.

weapons.name: Crusher Pole
weapons.special_rule: On crit: 25% chance of Concussion. Deals 15% bonus damage to Undead.
weapons.flavor: A weighted pole built for one purpose. Everything it hits it tries to break.

weapons.name: Spine Blade
weapons.special_rule: Serrated edges: 15% chance to apply Bleeding secondary on hit.
weapons.flavor: The blade is ridged and serrated along its length. Entry is bad. Removal is worse.

weapons.name: Bone Greatclub
weapons.flavor: Crafted from the largest bones of bog creatures. Heavy, crude, and surprisingly effective. A Swampbrood signature.

weapons.name: Jagged Greatblade
weapons.special_rule: Serrated: 15% chance to apply Bleeding secondary on hit.
weapons.flavor: A blade deliberately worked to be cruel. The teeth catch and tear. No surgeon enjoys treating these.

weapons.name: Twin Axes
weapons.special_rule: Paired weapons. Each turn, 25% chance to attack twice — once with each axe — at 70% damage each.
weapons.flavor: Two axes, one warrior. The left hand answers what the right starts. Fast, chaotic, and extremely hard to track.

weapons.name: Chain Blade
weapons.special_rule: The chain section can entangle: 15% chance to reduce target movement to 0 for 1 turn.
weapons.flavor: Blade at one end, chain at the other. Confusing to defend, exhausting to wield.

weapons.name: Meteor Hammer
weapons.special_rule: Can be swung on a chain to strike at 2-hex range (as a ranged arc throw, one use per battle, guaranteed crit, retrieved after). In melee: 15% chance of Staggering.
weapons.flavor: A weighted hammer at the end of a chain. In melee it is devastating. Thrown in an arc, it is a problem for someone two steps away.

weapons.name: Great Pick
weapons.special_rule: Treats target armour class as 4 lower when target wears plate or heavy armour.
weapons.flavor: Scaled up from a war pick. The full-body leverage punches through almost any surface.

weapons.name: Siege Hammer
weapons.special_rule: Deals double damage to structures, doors, and constructs. On hit: 30% chance of Concussion against living targets. On crit: guaranteed Stagger + Concussion.
weapons.flavor: This was not built to fight people. It was built to break siege gates. That it kills people is secondary — almost.

weapons.name: Hooked Polearm
weapons.special_rule: Hook: 25% chance to pull a rider from a mount, or drag a target 1 hex toward you.
weapons.flavor: The hook exists to catch, drag, and expose. A soldier pulled off their horse is in serious trouble.

weapons.name: Barbed Polearm
weapons.special_rule: Barbs: 20% chance of Bleeding secondary on hit. Targets hit must pass a Physiology check or suffer -10% movement next turn.
weapons.flavor: The barbs prevent withdrawal of the weapon — and what the weapon pierces. Intended to be permanent.

weapons.name: War Fork
weapons.special_rule: Two prongs: 20% chance to trap an enemy weapon between the tines on a hit, disarming for 1 turn.
weapons.flavor: The fork pins and catches. Shields can be pushed aside, weapons trapped. A trapping weapon as much as a stabbing one.

weapons.name: Heavy Lance
weapons.special_rule: Mounted charge: moving 2+ hexes in a straight line before hit triples damage (not doubles). Without a mount: -40% accuracy and -20% damage.
weapons.flavor: The heaviest lance made for warfare. On horseback at full gallop it has ended battles before they began.

weapons.name: Twinblade Sword
weapons.special_rule: Both ends carry a blade. Each hit has 20% chance of a bonus 50% damage reverse strike.
weapons.flavor: Two blades along a single shaft. Quick hands find twice as many openings.

weapons.name: Great Chain
weapons.special_rule: Reach 2 hexes. On hit: 25% chance to entangle the target — they cannot move or use reach weapons for 1 turn.
weapons.flavor: Nothing but heavy chain, wielded with whole-body rotation. Builds momentum and spreads impact across the full swing.

weapons.name: Spiked Great Chain
weapons.special_rule: Reach 2 hexes. On hit: 20% entangle chance. Spikes: 10% chance of Rupturing secondary on hit.
weapons.flavor: The spikes make the entangle worse and the blunt impact a secondary concern. Dreadful in every sense.
